channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Joe Biden going out same way as Aaron Rodgers
item.description: Bill O'Reilly believes outgoing president Joe Biden is "going out the same way" as New York Jets’ starting quarterback Aaron Rodgers.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Cortez Masto leads Nevada Democrats in renewed effort against nuclear waste dump
item.description: Nevada's Democratic U.S. senators and representatives in Congress reintroduced the Nuclear Waste Informed Consent Act on Wednesday, gearing up for possible renewed efforts to put a nuclear dump in the state.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with explosion of online porn
item.description: The Supreme Court weighed whether an explosion in online pornography requires repudiating the court's precedents concerning sexual content as the justices Wednesday heard arguments in a challenge to Texas’s age-verification law for porn websites.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: TikTok ban to impact local businesses, content creators
item.description: San Diego is home to talented and creative content creators who make a living on TikTok. While many are able to make a full-time income on the app, some are also helping small businesses in return, who say the app has inadvertently helped them tremendously.
item.dc:creator: Zara Barker, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis outlines immigration plan for lawmakers to consider ahead of special session
item.description: During a stop at the Polk County Sheriff’s Office, Gov. Ron DeSantis proposed a list of statewide immigration measures ahead of a special session.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: UN says it's ready to ramp up delivery of desperately needed aid to Gaza
item.description: The United Nations says it’s ready to ramp up the delivery of desperately needed humanitarian aid to Gaza after a ceasefire agreement and urged the removal of major security and political obstacles so it can reach all those in need.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: FDA bans red dye No. 3 from foods
item.description: U.S. regulators on Wednesday banned the dye called Red 3 from the nation’s food supply, nearly 35 years after it was barred from cosmetics because of potential cancer risk.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mike Johnson replaces powerful Intelligence Committee chairman after Russian nuclear threat warning
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson, R-La., is replacing Rep. Mike Turner, R-Ohio, as chairman of the House Intelligence Committee after a debacle that started with a warning about Russian space nuclear technology last year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Who is Trump's attorney general pick Pam Bondi? The former prosecutor is a close Trump ally
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump's pick to lead the Justice Department, Pam Bondi, faced questions on Capitol Hill Wednesday over her loyalty to the Republican president-elect, who has vowed to use the agency to pursue revenge on his perceived political enemies.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Group behind failed Florida recreational marijuana amendment trying again for 2026
item.description: The group behind the 2024 ballot measure that would have enshrined recreational marijuana access in the Florida Constitution is giving it another shot in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pam Bondi grilled over Trump's influence at AG hearing: 5 takeaways
item.description: Pam Bondi, President-elect Trump’s pick to lead the Department of Justice, echoed attacks on the agency she would lead if confirmed, but demurred on how she might handle tests of its independence. Bondi is a more traditional pick to lead the department than Trump’s initial choice, former Rep.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: End to daylight saving time among bills for 2025 Nevada Legislature
item.description: A Republican lawmaker who represents parts of six rural Nevada counties is sponsoring a bill to take the state off daylight saving time — one of the 200 bills written so far.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's pick for budget director won't say whether he would release Ukraine aid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Russell Vought, President-elect Donald Trump’s nominee for White House budget director, declined Wednesday to fully commit to doling out congressionally approved funds, specifically U.S. military aid to Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Head of US cybersecurity agency says she hopes it keeps up election work under Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Jen Easterly, the outgoing head of the U.S. government’s Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency, said Wednesday she hopes her agency is allowed to continue its election-related work under new leadership despite “contentiousness” around that part of its mission.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the special election for Vargas' Board of Supervisors seat
item.description: The San Diego County Board of Supervisors on Tuesday finalized plans to fill the seat formerly held by Nora Vargas before her abrupt exit from elected office through a special election.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Here's how to watch Biden's farewell address from the Oval Office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden will deliver his farewell address to the nation on Wednesday night, a speech designed to highlight his administration's accomplishments over the past four years.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Government agencies concerned Trump inauguration 'potential target' for extremists: report
item.description: Agencies are concerned that violent extremists may be drawn to President-elect Trump's inauguration, particularly those with "election-related grievances," according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Duffy confirmation hearing marked by bipartisanship, pledge to visit Helene-devastated states
item.description: Former Rep. Sean Duffy sat for an hourslong confirmation hearing as President-elect Donald Trump's pick for Transportation secretary, sitting before Chairman Sen. Ted Cruz and Ranking Member Maria Cantwell.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AG nominee Pam Bondi won't say Trump lost 2020 election
item.description: Attorney general nominee Pam Bondi dodged a question from Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) on Wednesday about whether President-elect Trump lost the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Live coverage: Biden's farewell speech comes on heels of raucous confirmation hearings
item.description: President Biden delivered a somewhat grim primetime farewell speech on Wednesday from the Oval Office to mark the final days of his presidency.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, NewsNation

item.title: Trump will begin his presidency in delicate position, poll finds
item.description: A new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll shows that Americans' support for President-elect Donald Trump's top priorities is split, despite his claims of a mandate for his agenda.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota legislative standoff spills out of the Statehouse into the courthouse
item.description: With accusations flying over which party is ignoring state law, the fight to control the Minnesota House is shifting from lawmakers to lawyers. DFL Secretary of State Steve Simon is calling on the Minnesota Supreme Court to weigh in on who has what power.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: House barrels toward fight over placing conditions on California fire aid
item.description: The House is barreling toward a contentious debate over disaster relief for California as many — but not all — Republicans call for conditions to be placed on the assistance, an idea Democrats are soundly rejecting. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Blinken makes the case for post-war reconstruction, security and governance of Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken made a last-minute case Tuesday for a plan for the post-war reconstruction and governance of Gaza as a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas appears tantalizingly close to completion.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams addresses migrants, crime and political future
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Mayor Eric Adams had a wide-ranging press conference Monday discussing everything from closing down migrant shelters, to subway safety, to his tough reelection battle.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Special counsel in Hunter Biden case denounces president for criticism of the probe
item.description: In a final report on his investigation, special counsel David Weiss said President Biden's criticism "undermines the very foundation of what makes America's justice system fair and equitable.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Hegseth’s views on women in combat, infidelity and more
item.description: As author of several books and a former Fox News Channel host, Hegseth has been forced to defend himself against a long record of his own public comments, including in his most recent book, “The War on Warriors: Behind the Betrayal of the Men Who Keep Us Free.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Newsom concerned Trump will try to withhold California fire aid
item.description: “He’s done it in Utah. He’s done it in Michigan, did it in Puerto Rico. He did it to California back before I was even governor, in 2018, until he found out folks in Orange County voted for him and he decided to give them money.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5

item.title: Despite Trump's win, 'election integrity' activists still seek sweeping voting changes
item.description: Republican voters say they trust the 2024 election was administered well, yet pro-Trump conservatives are pushing some sweeping reforms to voting systems.
item.dc:creator: Miles Parks, MPR News

item.title: DFL, GOP jockey for power in Minnesota House a day before session’s start
item.description: House Republicans ripped DFLers over tactics that could keep the House from conducting business Tuesday. Melissa Hortman, the House’s top DFLer, defended her moves, telling MPR News that denying a quorum is “the only negotiating power I have right now.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Nevada's Cortez Masto to reintroduce bill to fund fight against wildfires
item.description: In the wake of the devastating California wildfires, Nevada Democratic Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto plans to reintroduce a proposal in Congress to better fund fire departments, interagency coordination and recovery efforts.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada Democrats join Republicans in bill to detain migrants accused of certain crimes
item.description: All five members of Southern Nevada’s congressional delegation said they support a Republican proposal to detain immigrants in the country illegally who commit certain crimes.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
